texto: The pursuit of material-external things as if they can provide deep and lasting happiness is contributing to a dwindling of real meaning and is a factor contributing to an increase in mental health problems. It just isn't possible to fill the hole inside us by piling up the things outside us." pg9
autor: Dr Stephen McKenzie and Dr Craig Hassed
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Dr Stephen McKenzie and Dr Craig Hassed: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “The pursuit of material-external things as if they can provide deep and lasting happiness is cont…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, happiness, material-things pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de material-things e mindfulness.
